name: Firmware Integrity Check
description: Validates firmware integrity on managed endpoints by comparing current firmware versions and hashes against known-good baselines. When a firmware drift is detected, Riggs AI performs an insider threat assessment to determine if the change is malicious (bootkit/rootkit) or a legitimate vendor update. Unauthorized changes trigger host isolation, a P1 incident ticket, and security leadership notification. Authorized updates are documented and closed.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Query firmware version and isolate endpoint if firmware is compromised

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create P1 incident ticket for unauthorized firmware modifications

config.description: Triggers when a firmware version or hash deviates from the known-good baseline

config.code:
current_version = alert.get('firmware_version', '')
current_hash = alert.get('firmware_hash', '')
baseline_version = alert.get('baseline_version', '')
baseline_hash = alert.get('baseline_hash', '')
vendor = alert.get('firmware_vendor', 'Unknown')
version_changed = current_version != baseline_version
hash_changed = current_hash != baseline_hash
result = {'current_version': current_version, 'baseline_version': baseline_version, 'current_hash': current_hash, 'hash_changed': hash_changed, 'version_changed': version_changed, 'vendor': vendor, 'drift_detected': version_changed or hash_changed}
config.description: Compare the current firmware version and hash against the known-good baseline

config.additional_instructions: Analyze this firmware change for signs of bootkit/rootkit implantation or supply chain compromise. Determine if the change matches a known vendor update or represents unauthorized modification. Consider firmware vendor, version progression, and timing.
config.description: AI-powered analysis to determine if the firmware change is a legitimate vendor update or malicious modification
Routes based on whether the AI determined the firmware change is unauthorized
Immediately isolate the endpoint to prevent potential bootkit/rootkit from spreading

parameters.title: P1: Unauthorized Firmware Modification — {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}}

config.description: Create a P1 critical incident ticket for the unauthorized firmware modification
Alert security leadership about the potential firmware compromise

parameters.note: Firmware change on {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}} reviewed. Change from {{code_1.baseline_version}} to {{code_1.current_version}} matches known {{code_1.vendor}} vendor update. Updating baseline.

config.description: Document the legitimate vendor firmware update and update the baseline
